We have, yes.

Okay, that's okay.
How have you been?
Doing well.
It's, yeah, nothing too crazy.
But you've been traveling, right?
Or you were in Budapest?
I was.
I was in Budapest.
I'm now in Peru.
So that's why my background has changed once again.
So, yeah, I am back in Lisbon at the end of this month.
So, yeah, it's been good.
Wow.
We were just in Lisbon.
Oh, really?
Yeah, for your offsite.
For an offsite, yeah.
Yeah, yeah.
How was it?
Hey Mark, how was it?
It did.
Got a lot done.
It was nice to see the events that the team puts on.
And I think it was a really productive time.
Nice.
How are you, Mark?
I'm good.
How are you?
Good, good.
Thanks.
Mara is going to be a few minutes late.
She's had a call running over.
So are we waiting on someone?
Can we get started?
No, we can get started.
All right.
Awesome.
Okay.
Hopefully everyone can see my screen.
Yeah.
Sorry, I'm still getting used to Zoom a bit, so.
Oh, okay.
So just running for the updates real quick.
We have 46 articles ready for your review in Airtable.
We're on track to deliver eight new this week, and we addressed your edits.
We published six last week.
And I think following last week's discussions about the columns and the people for reviews, we've added two columns.
to Airtable.
So one is just a rapid internal reviewer.
Once you tell us the names of who you wish to get added, we'll add them to Airtable, and then you can either tag them in there, and, you know, we'll follow up in Slack regarding notifications.
I can do that on this call.
I don't see that column in here, though.
It might be a view.
So let me just open Airtable.
Show me where that column is, and I can give you the names of all the people that should go on it in this meeting.
Sure.
So can you see this view, or?
I'm on Assignments, yeah, I'm in that view.
You can't see it?
Yeah, well, I can't see.
I just don't.
I see on your screen, I see Rapid Internal Reviewer under the Assignments tab.
I'm under the Assignments tab right now, and I don't So can you click All Records over here under the More Collaborative Views section?
Oh, no.
All Records.
Okay, yeah.
Let me go to that.
All
There it is.
Okay, now I see it.
So there's two columns.

Then Rapid Internal Reviewers, yeah, you could just let us know.
If you just give us a list of people, we can sort of add them and then you can tag them over here.
Do you need email and name, right?
We just need emails.
You just need emails.
Nicolette, as we're chatting here, you know who all those people are, right?
Can you compile those emails real quick and pop them in the chat for Vivek?
Yeah, this is just the Solutions Teams and then Ari.
It'll be Solutions, you, Vanessa, me, Lizzie, and also McKay, Christopher, McDuffin on Andrew's team.
Okay.
If you wouldn't mind just putting together that list of emails real quick.
You can

Cool.
All right.
So, yeah, those were the updates.
Moving on to the performance update side of things.
Just a caveat with this week's numbers.
Google's reporting is a bit of a mess right now.
Basically, what happened is they introduced an update last week where, you know, they disabled an option to view 100 results on one page.
So, it seems like a pretty simple thing.
Oh, disabled that?
They did, yeah.
So, you can't add the operator in the search bar anymore.
They just not want people to, like, use their freaking reporting anymore?
Probably.
The repercussions have been mostly for the keyword search tools because now it's going to cost them a lot more to pick Google and retrieve search results.
But, you know, you wouldn't think that would happen in a...
And, like, impact on reporting, but unfortunately, it seems to have had it.
We aren't seeing that much with Rapids data, but we have seen generally click numbers, impressions, you know, session numbers, views, et cetera, just going absolutely berserk.
Even positions, in fact, I think are pretty much incorrect.
They seem to have combined it with another update to sort of curb bot traffic.
So it's generally GSC and GA4 data is all over the place right now.
So with that caveat, I present this chart.
It seems like we're still hanging around the all-time high of last week, which is pretty normal.
Usually you hit new highs and then go on to a sort of a plateau, and then you make another high, et cetera.
Generally, we noticed first a bunch of referrals from Yandex, which was interesting.
We are in the first position for...
542 non-branded keywords.
But again, this data is a bit iffy right now because it comes from GSC.
On a cluster level, as I mentioned, position level tracking is showing a lot of volatility.
Like over the last week, I think it showed something like 15% to 30% drops in position, which we don't really trust just from manual Googling and in cognitive mode.
It seems like we're pretty much holding positions, but yeah, it's best to just wait for a few weeks and see how this thing smooths out.
In terms of LLM traffic, we saw a slight drop from last week's peak.
Perplexity is again the highest referrer, followed by GPT.
And another update is we had created a bunch of prompts that we believe are relevant for Rapids business, and we uploaded them to Scrunch, which is, you know, our AI LLM observability tool.
So Scrunch usually takes about two to three.
weeks or even a month sometimes just to gather data and show us where we're showing up for which queries, et cetera.
So we'll have that data for you in the following weeks.
So that was something we did in terms of performance tracking as well.
Any questions I can answer about any of this?
Could we just take a look at that LLM traffic?
Sure.
Can we pop up that table and look at it?
Yep.
So this is basically, it's still loading, but yeah, this is the latest week.
So the previous week, we had this sort of high.
This is only for the blogs that we have created, by the way, this is not the entire website.
Yeah.
If I check this, you know, it shows the entire website.
But yeah, we're just off the highs.
So you can see GPT here is the highest followed by perplexity.
This is for the entire time period.
I'm Just looking at Gemini, it's like.
It looks like we had like a Gemini golden age that lasted for like two weeks there and then kind of went away.
Does that coincide with like losing ranking on some specific blog posts or anything?
Or is there any way to get an idea of why?
From what we know, Gemini doesn't really coincide with search rankings as much.
You'd think it would, but it doesn't seem to be the case.
It's still a bit random.
The truth is, you know, there's no definitive answer to this yet because we just don't have the data and nobody's sharing that data.
All we know is that generally good SEO performance is awarded across all LLMs.
But to say that, you know, bad SEO, I mean, how do I put this correctly?
A drop in Gemini does not mean we're dropping in SEO.
So there's no equivalence over there.
So, yeah.
Okay.
I was just, is there.
Any way to know, like, what was driving any of that green big bar there in Gemini?
This one?
Seems like there's a lot more Gemini traffic there for a little bit.
Yes, Crunch will give us some idea, but unfortunately for this time period, we don't have that data, and Google doesn't share that data either.
In fact, none of the LLM platforms share it.
So really, a lot of it is just kind of like guessing.
One way of reverse engineering it is just looking at these lists of landing pages and seeing, you know, I mean, you can kind of guess, like, maybe what questions came in here.
So, for example, if I just click Gemini over here for this, just that little bit, we can see which pages show up.
Yeah, we won't get the exact queries or prompts, but we can kind of see which pages show up.
Looker is a bit slow usually, so this will take a...
Maybe a couple of minutes to load.
But yeah, that's kind of how this works.
Seems like it's taking a while to load.
I think we can come back to this if you're okay with it because there were some other things that I wanted can also dig into this later.
was just curious.
I am interested when we're going to start seeing Scrunch reports.
Yeah, so we loaded it.
Last week.
And it usually takes about three to four weeks to sort of ping the LLMs and then gather enough data for presentation.

Okay.
We are proceeding with the competitor sort of articles, the comparison articles for high opportunity sectors.

next Thank

N-U-V-E-I, yeah.

Airwallex.
Isn't Airwallex more of an account?

I guess if we're just going to focus on comparisons for payment processing, no.

Like, it's a commodity and they're bigger than us.
It's that simple.

And we, you know, it's like, it's all those little like nuance features that actually are where Rapid stands out from these guys.

It's in all those little details where we have our value proposition.
And I shouldn't say, oh, they're better than us and everything.
It's not necessarily true.
But it is just hard to make that case and where you make that case is on those other services.
So that's why, you know, like, I can see Airwallex coming in.
But I get your point, too, that nobody's looking for Airwallex for, like, their high-risk payment processing.
So instead of saying payment processing, can we say something like payment solutions?
Yeah, absolutely.
that goes better.
I mean, this is a good list to start with.
can give you, there are a couple other ones, like trust payments.
In the UK, might be one to add to the list.
Okay.
Payable.
Okay.
Is it?

P-A-B-L.
Yeah, P-A-Y-A-B Yeah.
I think that's a good list.
Okay.
All right, we'll get started with these and then, you know, as and when more competitors come out.
I'm curious, is there, is GoCardless also, can we include them, or?
I don't know much about them.
So I, I can't say.
Okay.
You could add here, though, tunes in Asia would be another one.
How do you spell that, sorry?
It is spelled T-H-U-N-E-S.
Tunes.
Okay.
And then in terms of the process for this, like we gave you our competitor.
gonna
I know it's not terribly helpful.
What would actually be really helpful for me is if after you're doing this, if you could put together questions for me.
You know, if you're looking at artifacts for this or whatever, you're like, hey, need to know X, Y, Z.
Like just having like a document where it's like, okay, I need to go find the answers to this, this, this, and this would be helpful for me.
Yeah.
So we put together this template.
And basically, this is kind of how we see the article just living on page.
There are a few.
So the way I thought about this is like, okay, we have a bunch of H2s, which are basically your comparison dimensions.
And then, you we throw some tables on the page.
Wanted to get your thoughts on, first of all, are these dimensions, do they make sense?
Are they sensitive, et cetera?
And I'll share this in Slack as well.
But I've left some comments in terms of like, you know, some of the things that we want to highlight over here.
Since we've moved away from payment processing and more to payments,
I think this section here, key features and use cases might need to expand more to like, you know, there's merchant acquiring, there's accounts as well.
We can add that over here.
But yeah, if it's just highlighted some of the things that we need from Vue, et cetera.
So happy to talk through this if you think this is good.
One nuance, I would do up to approval rate and speed.

So I like to call this onboarding approval rate and speed.
Like, just always clarify that one.
Otherwise, it can get confusing.
Okay.
And then there are some other things I would add here.
So why don't you share this list with me and I can add some stuff in and even provide some context around each of these.
Sure.
Yeah, I've linked to it in the agenda and I will send this in Slack as well.
Great.
Yeah.
It's not super long, so hopefully if this becomes too much of a heavy lift, please do let us know.
We will find other ways to, like, get your input.
But yeah.
In terms of, like, the messaging around this, you know, like, I think these are good comparison features.
How are you going to get the messaging around it?
Do we need to provide any details there, too?
What we're going to do is basically take messaging from publicly available sources.
And then during editing, if you have any additional information, you can either add it in or, you know, we'll generate one sample page, right?
And then if you have any additional internal information, we...
can always add that to our artifacts for articles moving forward.
It might be helpful to put together like a little sheet that has key points about some of these things.
Vanessa, do you have some time?
I'm in a conference already.
I'm actually doing a GEO conference today that I'm taking time out of.
I was listening to Neil Patel before I jumped on here.
Would you have time, Vanessa, to take a pass through this document and just like make sure that we're, you know, you're proving for all of our like benefits and features and capabilities.
Make sure that we're talking like in these points, calling out kind of the right talking points for Rapyd.
Yeah.
And maybe track changes and then share with me also.
But I'd love somebody to just kind of take a pass through it.
Like, for instance, I can see here, we don't want to talk about processing fees at all.
That's like.
Great.
That's what I was thinking.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And it's.
I would see the other things highlighted, like reserve requirements and volume limits.
We don't really talk about those.
disclose, yeah.
We talk about security features a little bit, you know, high level.
But, okay, great.
Yeah, I can do that.
Cool.
If the processing fees, I know you don't want to compete on processing fees, but the reason we're including it here is because just from a UX perspective, people will expect to know about that.

So, you know, just throwing it out there, even if it is, even if Rapyd is a little more expensive than competitors, or you don't want people to be judging you based on that, we can find ways to minimize it on page.
But just from a UX perspective, like if I'm evaluating a processor, the first thing that comes into mind is, okay, what's the interchange?
What's the fees?
What's the hit to my, you know, revenue per transaction, right?
So, you would think...
You would think so, but it's like when you're working with these high opportunity industries, it gets very different.
It becomes less about like how much are you going to charge me and more about like will you onboard me and keep me processing and then we will charge them a very high premium to do that.
Okay.
Like whereas if you look at the like the processing fees for like high opportunity industries and like this isn't just rapid, like this is across the industry.
Like if you're Walmart, you might be getting charged like a 2.5% transaction fee on each Visa transaction.
If you're DraftKings, you're getting charged 30% on every transaction, like literally like orders of magnitude above what somebody else is paying.
And it's, that's just like how it is in the industry.
So it's just, and like all of that is done like at a negotiating table between like salespeople and like the people there.
It's, there is no like.
Okay.
rate sheet.
it's just not something that you talk about because it's super, it's just, like, fees are not the main thing in these industries.
Okay.
even, like, Apple's even more than that.
Like, if you were going to try to, like, do something like this through the App Store, they charge a crazy high rate.
Okay.
That was good info.
Wow.
All right.

Okay.

Latam.

Vanessa, thanks for taking a look at that article.
I like this update on the competitors here.

seeing a lot better articles in there.
It's been easier to get through them.

Okay.
Yeah.

All right.
That's all I got.
All right.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Rapyd and GrowthX to discuss content strategy, performance updates, and competitor analysis for payment solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [46 articles ready for review; 8 new articles to be delivered this week](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=102.0)
  - [Google's reporting issues causing data volatility; current performance metrics unreliable](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Competitor comparison articles being developed; list of competitors refined](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Need to develop detailed personas to tailor content more effectively](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1376.0)

## Topics

### Content Production and Review Process

  - [46 articles ready for review in Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=102.0)
  - [8 new articles to be delivered this week](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=102.0)
  - [6 articles published last week](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=110.0)
  - [New columns added in Airtable: "Rapid Internal Reviewer" and "Good for Social"](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=112.0)
  - [List of internal reviewers to be compiled and added to Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=217.0)

### Performance Updates

  - [Google Search Console (GSC) and GA4 data unreliable due to recent Google updates](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Traffic seems to be maintaining near all-time high levels](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [542 non-branded keywords in first position (data potentially unreliable)](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [LLM traffic slightly down from previous week's peak](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=393.0)
  - [Perplexity remains highest LLM referrer, followed by GPT](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
  - [Scrunch tool implementation for AI/LLM observability (results expected in 2-3 weeks)](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)

### Competitor Analysis for Payment Solutions

  - [Refined list of competitors: Adyen, PayPal, Stripe, Nuvei, WorldPay (FIS), Trust Payments, Payable, Thunes](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Shifting focus from "payment processing" to broader "payment solutions"](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Template created for comparison articles, including dimensions like onboarding approval rate and speed, security features, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=646.0)
  - [Processing fees discussion to be minimized due to industry-specific pricing complexities](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1200.0)

### Content Strategy

  - [Prioritizing stablecoin and competitor articles](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)
  - [LATAM articles and refreshes of older content in the pipeline](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1319.0)
  - [Need to develop detailed personas to tailor content more effectively](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1376.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Nicolette to compile and share list of internal reviewer emails](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=217.0)
  - [Vanessa to review and refine competitor comparison article template](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1124.0)
  - [Team to prioritize Stablecoin and LATAM articles for review next week](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1347.0)
  - [Mark to work on developing detailed personas for content tailoring](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1376.0)
  - [GrowthX to generate first draft of competitor comparison article for review](https://fathom.video/share/CeUbXxAe-TysFuDj92rHAJ6bfykbm6aH?tab=summary&timestamp=1289.0)

action_items.description: Compile list of emails for Rapid Internal Reviewers (Solutions Teams, Ari, Vanessa, Mark, Lizzie, McKay, Christopher, McDuffin) and send to Vivek via Slack
Add additional context and dimensions to competitor comparison document after Vanessa's review

Review competitor comparison document, ensure Rapyd benefits/features/capabilities highlighted, remove sensitive info (e.g. processing fees, reserve requirements, volume limits), track changes, share with Mark
